Notes: Battle format: boss character + 2 SR actives + 4 reserves. Boss level 12, team at floor level 10. Move slots: 1 signature / 2 secondary damage / 3-4 support. Boss art deviations (approved): 1024x1536 portrait, signature implements allowed (no-props rule waived).
Move_1: Reap the Fleeing (NIGHTMARE) · P160 CD3 · Damage; +50% more vs targets with a reduced Speed stage.
Move_2: Run-Down (DUSTHOUND) · P120 CD2 · Damage; Harried: target -1 Speed stage, 1t.
Move_3: Wild Command (HERNE) · P0 CD2 · One ally +20% Offense, 2t; Beast allies gain +30% instead.

Kit_Synergy: Pack-hunt execution loop: Run-Down strips Speed, Reap the Fleeing punishes it for +50%. Wild Command feeds the all-Beast reserve pack (+30%), while Ziz's Unbroken Gale keeps the whole team acting first and Hart sustains with Sovereign's Bloom.
Description: The first lord of the tower — a feral huntsman-king who rules the meadow's packs from a claw-scored den, crowned in the skull of the last thing that challenged him.
Image_Prompt: A towering feral huntsman-king nearly three meters tall, corded muscle beneath layered wolf and bear pelts, wearing the skull of a great horned predator as a helm with solid yellow eyes glinting inside the sockets, necklaces of trophy fangs and claws across a scarred chest, both hands ending in dark iron claw-gauntlets. As a Beast creature he also shows thick fur and hide bristling across his shoulders, a fanged predatory snarl beneath the skull-helm, and a feral forward-hunched stance ready to lunge. On a plain flat pure-white seamless backdrop, just the character and nothing else — NO floor, NO ground plane, NO surface, NO ground-cast shadow, the figure is not standing on anything. Full body, a towering humanoid figure, seen from a three-quarter angle, NOT looking at the camera. The figure occupies only the CENTER 70% of the frame with generous empty background on all sides — nothing even close to any edge. Richly saturated vivid colors, dramatic dark-fantasy rim lighting with deep shadows, moody painterly rendering, NOT washed out, NOT overexposed, one single cohesive figure, no text, no scenery, no floating or detached parts.

Anoint in Fire hits Ignifer at the full +40% Elemental rate while Ferrodrake tanks the reprisal — two forge-born dragons defending the machine still building itself.
